steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
The radius is the distance from $C(h,k)$ to $A(h+2,k+\sqrt{237})$.
$$r=\sqrt{(h+2-h)^2+(k+\sqrt{237}-k)^2}$$
$$r=\sqrt{2^2+(\sqrt{237})^2}=\sqrt{241}$$

steps.title: Use the right angle
steps.content:
Both $CA$ and $CB$ are radii, so each has length $\sqrt{241}$. Since angle $ACB$ is a right angle, triangle $ACB$ has legs $CA$ and $CB$.
$$AB^2=(\sqrt{241})^2+(\sqrt{241})^2=482$$

steps.title: Find the chord length
steps.content:
A length is positive, so
$$AB=\sqrt{482}$$
The answer is D.
